ddls:
CREATE TABLE "publications" (
  "publication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each publication */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the publication */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the publication was released */,
  "source_title" TEXT /* Title of the publication source (e.g., journal, conference) */,
  "doi" TEXT /* Digital Object Identifier (DOI) for the publication */,
  "cited_by" INTEGER /* Number of times the publication has been cited */,
  "document_type" TEXT /* Type of document (e.g., Article, Conference Paper) */,
  "access_type" INTEGER /* Type of access to the publication (e.g., open access, subscription-based) */,
  "abstract" TEXT /* A brief summary of the publication */,
  "keywords" TEXT /* Keywords associated with the publication */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* The name of the publisher of the publication */,
  "volume" TEXT /* The volume number of the publication */,
  "issue" TEXT /* The issue number of the publication */,
  "pages" TEXT /* The page range of the publication */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* The exact date of publication */,
  "language" TEXT /* The language in which the publication is written */,
  "funding_source" TEXT /* Information about the funding sources for the research */,
  "research_field" TEXT /* The field of research the publication belongs to */,
  "impact_factor" REAL /* The impact factor of the publication source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("publication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Institutional affiliation of the author */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the author is based */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "orcid" TEXT /* ORCID identifier of the author */,
  "research_interests" TEXT /* A brief description of the author's research interests */,
  "h_index" INTEGER /* The H-index of the author */,
  "role" TEXT /* The role of the author (e.g., lead author, corresponding author) */,
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the user_roles table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_authors_user_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("user_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "author_publication" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author */,
  "publication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the publication */,
  "author_order" INTEGER /* Order of the author in the publication */,
  "contribution" TEXT /* A brief description of the author's contribution to the publication */,
  "corresponding_author" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the author is the corresponding author */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id", "publication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "institutions" (
  "institution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each institution */,
  "institution_name" TEXT /* Name of the institution */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the institution is located */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the institution is located */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the institution */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the institution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("institution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, author, administrator) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object or text field describing the permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "citations" (
  "citation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each citation */,
  "cited_publication_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the publications table (the publication being cited) */,
  "citing_publication_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the publications table (the publication that cites another) */,
  "citation_date" TEXT /* The date when the citation was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("citation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_citations_cited_publication_id FOREIGN KEY ("cited_publication_id") REFERENCES publications ("publication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_citations_citing_publication_id FOREIGN KEY ("citing_publication_id") REFERENCES publications ("publication_id")
)
